skills: topic selection for broad AI productivity interest
domain_experience: AI productivity content and audience interests
assets: X account with about 120000 followers interested in AI productivity
distribution: Large X audience in AI productivity
constraints: Audience appears concentrated in students and hobbyists
No evidence yet of owned buyer list or conversion data
credibility_signals: Large public following in a specific topic category
unfair_advantages: Can test messaging quickly with a large existing audience interested in AI productivity

usable_edge: Large top-of-funnel reach in AI productivity
Proven ability to attract audience attention in the niche
fake_or_overstated_edge: Follower count treated as proof of buyer access
Audience interest treated as purchasing intent
AI productivity popularity treated as durable moat
weak_spots: No evidence of paying customer behavior from the audience
Likely low purchasing power concentration if audience is mostly students and hobbyists
No warm buyer network or owned demand channel evidenced
follow_up_questions: What percentage of your audience has budget authority or business purchasing intent?
Have you sold anything to this audience before, and what converted?
Which posts attract comments or DMs from professionals versus learners?
Do you have an email list, community, or customer base separate from X?
Which buyer segment do you know best personally: founders, teams, creators, agencies, or students?
confidence_notes: Medium confidence that the audience is a real distribution asset because the follower count is explicit.
High confidence that buyer quality is unproven because the user explicitly says most followers are students and hobbyists and they do not know if any are buyers.
Low confidence on network access, conversion potential, and monetization paths because no evidence was provided.
